purpose: Help the user pick a restaurant fast, with neighborhood context, key chips (price, cuisine, vibe), and quick booking detail.
primaryUserGoal: Decide where to eat tonight (or for a specific occasion) and see what's worth booking.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks for restaurant recommendations, dinner options, where to eat, or a shortlist of places in a neighborhood.
goodFor: Tonight's dinner in a specific city or neighborhood
Restaurant shortlists for a visiting friend
supports: Grouped list of restaurants by neighborhood
Detail panel with hours, website, phone, cuisine as plain label+value rows
idealDataShape: Per-restaurant details: hours, website link, phone, cuisine

populationInstructions.summary: Wrap all content in a tabs section (Results / Saved / Notes). The Results tab pane holds a balanced split: grouped-list on the left, detail-panel on the right. Do NOT add a filter-strip anywhere.
steps: Create a top-level 'tabs' section with three tabs: Results, Saved, Notes. Set activeTabId to the Results tab id.
Place the entire split section INSIDE the Results tab pane — never at the top level outside tabs.
Use ratio: 'balanced' on the split so both columns are equal width.
Group 5-7 restaurants by neighborhood in a grouped-list on the left side; tone-code top picks with success.
On the right side, populate a detail-panel for the initially selected restaurant with these fields stacked as plain rows: Hours, Website (as a link), Phone, Cuisine.
Keep per-item subtitle short (cuisine + price tier); put the full description in the detail panel.
Do NOT add a filter-strip section — omit all filter chips.
guardrails: ALL sections must be nested inside tab panes — never place sections at the top level outside the tabs section.
Do NOT add a filter-strip. Omit filters entirely.
Don't make up hours or addresses — if unknown, say 'check website'.
Don't claim booking availability unless verified.
Keep to one cuisine-dense neighborhood per group.

patchPrefer: Use upsert_groups to add or swap restaurants in a neighborhood
Use set_detail when the user picks a different restaurant
replaceTriggers: User switches from dinner to a different meal context
